item.text: I’ll use Zap, then retake the center with raiders: remove the 1 HP marksman, put capped damage into the forward P2 raider, and recruit another attacker.

item.command:
/bin/zsh -lc 'set -euo pipefail
RUN=.games/e024-codex-rush-vs-engine-guided
cat > "$RUN/actions/turn-013.deck.json" <<'"'JSON'
{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-013\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":[{\"type\":\"playAction\",\"handIndex\":2},{\"type\":\"moveToBuy\"},{\"type\":\"endTurn\"}]}
JSON
bun run --silent cli -- deck-turn --config rulesets/territory-v1-cost6-damagecap-responsewin-lead4-highmove-center6/deck.yaml --state \""'$RUN/deck.json" --actions "$RUN/actions/turn-013.deck.json" --result "$RUN/results/turn-013.deck.result.json"
cat > "$RUN/actions/turn-013.board.json" <<'"'JSON'
{\"schemaVersion\":1,\"turnId\":\"turn-013\",\"player\":\"P1\",\"actions\":{\"movements\":[{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-turn-009-1\",\"from\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":3},\"to\":{\"col\":6,\"row\":5}},{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-turn-009-2\",\"from\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":2},\"to\":{\"col\":7,\"row\":5}},{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-turn-011-1\",\"from\":{\"col\":10,\"row\":1},\"to\":{\"col\":8,\"row\":3}}],\"recruits\":[{\"unit\":\"P1-raider-turn-013-1\",\"type\":\"raider\",\"at\":{\"col\":10,\"row\":1}}],\"attacks\":[{\"attacker\":\"P1-raider-turn-009-1\",\"target\":\"P2-marksman-start-4\",\"deckDamage\":0},{\"attacker\":\"P1-raider-turn-009-2\",\"target\":\"P2-raider-turn-008-1\",\"deckDamage\":1}],\"heals\":[],\"upgrades\":[]}}
JSON
bun run --silent cli -- board-turn --state \""'$RUN/board.json" --deck-result "$RUN/results/turn-013.deck.result.json" --actions "$RUN/actions/turn-013.board.json" --result "$RUN/results/turn-013.board.result.json"
bun run --silent playtest -- commit-turn --run "$RUN" --deck-result "$RUN/results/turn-013.deck.result.json" --board-result "$RUN/results/turn-013.board.result.json" --summary "P1 played Zap, retook the center, removed P2'"'s wounded marksman, damaged a forward raider, and recruited another raider.\" --reasoning \"RUSH plan: use Zap for immediate pressure, convert existing raiders into center attacks, eliminate a nearly dead ranged unit, soften the next melee threat, and keep spending supply on board presence.\" --strict-win
bun run --silent validate-run -- --strict --strict-deck --strict-win \""'$RUN/timeline.json"
printf '"'\\nDECK_FILE\\n'
jq -c '.' \""'$RUN/results/turn-013.deck.result.json"
printf '"'BOARD_FILE\\n'
jq -c '.' \""'$RUN/results/turn-013.board.result.json"'

item.text: The center hex was not adjacent enough for the marksman attack under this grid’s range calculation. I’m shifting that raider one hex farther west while preserving the same kill-and-pressure plan.

item.text:
Committed and validated `turn-013`.

Deck line: played `zap`, moved to buy, ended turn. Bought nothing. Produced `5` money and `1` damage.

Board line: moved raiders to `(5,5)`, `(7,5)`, and `(8,3)`, recruited `P1-raider-turn-013-1` at `(10,1)`, removed `P2-marksman-start-4`, and dealt `7` damage to `P2-raider-turn-008-1`.

Rationale: this keeps tempo with Zap, removes the wounded ranged threat, pressures P2’s forward raider, and adds another raider to sustain the rush. Strict validation passed.
